rule: hit = kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; kw is strict-substring count of content_words in title+snippet

results.reason: Source-domain reference about velvet worm biology; no LLM defense mechanism described.
Adversarial preference training for alignment; no downstream projection or two-phase hardening.
Adaptive attack analysis bypassing defenses; not a downstream-projected adhesive defense.
One-shot steering is precomputed but source-time/first-shot, not downstream-projected with two-phase trigger.
ProAct proactive defense fires at classifier output (source-time), not at predicted downstream position with hardening transition.
Adversarial activation patching at downstream layer locations is mechanistically adjacent to downstream-projected defense.
Context-window activation intervention; not specifically downstream-projected with liquid-to-solid trigger.
Layer-spread defense with precomputed gradient injected at later layer where attack manifests is functionally near-equivalent to SLIME-NET's downstream-projected two-phase defense.

fail_reason: Two functional collisions: Layer-Spread Defense (rank 8, func 0.82) and Adversarial Activation Patching Across Layers (rank 6, func 0.78) both describe downstream-layer-projected defense that is functionally near-equivalent to SLIME-NET's downstream-projection mechanism, even though they lack explicit two-phase liquid-to-solid hardening.
